steps.title: Identify the key contrast
steps.content: The claim requires evidence that society prefers familiarity over novelty. The strongest signal would be a quotation showing the society choosing something familiar despite a newer, more impressive alternative being available. This direct comparison would best illustrate the preference.

steps.title: Apply the contrast to the choices
steps.content: Choice D directly shows the contrast: despite talk of a new Opera House designed to compete with European capitals, the world of fashion remains “still content to reassemble every winter” in the shabby old Academy of Music, rejecting the novel option for the familiar one. Choices A, B, and C address social conventions, emphasize novelty rather than familiarity, or discuss arrival methods rather than venue preference. That matches choice D — D is correct.
